$comment: Registry of every {{placeholder}} allowed in templates/. The interview step of big-emotion-setup collects these values. check-templates.mjs fails the build on any placeholder not declared here. GitHub Actions expressions (${{ ... }}) and Jira smart values ({{issue.key}} — anything with a dot) are not placeholders.
project_slug: Kebab-case project short name; skill prefix and package name (e.g. acme-support-agent)
project_display_name: Human-readable project name (e.g. Acme support agent)
default_branch: Repository default branch (usually main); release tags land here
package_manager: npm or pnpm; templates ship in pnpm form, adaptation guidance in references
package_manager_version: Pinned package-manager version for the packageManager field (e.g. pnpm@10.12.1)
node_version: Node major version for engines and setup-node (e.g. 24)
jira_issue_type_id_epic: Jira issue type id for Epic
jira_issue_type_id_story: Jira issue type id for Story
jira_issue_type_id_task: Jira issue type id for Task
jira_issue_type_id_bug: Jira issue type id for Bug
jira_issue_type_id_subtask: Jira issue type id for Sub-task
confluence_root_page_id: Confluence page id the spec tree roots at
confluence_requirements_page_id: Confluence page id for Requirements (set by bootstrap)
confluence_decisions_page_id: Confluence page id for Decisions (set by bootstrap)
confluence_architecture_page_id: Confluence page id for Architecture (set by bootstrap)
confluence_obsolete_page_id: Confluence page id for Obsolete (set by bootstrap)
deploy_workflow: Filename of the production deploy workflow (e.g. deploy-production.yml)
ferry_jira_account: Email or account id of the dedicated ferry Jira user
working_branch_prefix: Prefix for human working branches (e.g. feat/)
